title: Challenges and Opportunities for Universal Health Coverage in South Asia: A Scoping Review
authors: Jayendra Sharma, Milena Pavlova, Wim Groot
description: Scoping review examining challenges and opportunities for UHC across South Asian countries, identifying underfunding, fragmented health systems, and inadequate insurance coverage as key barriers.

title: Extending Universal Health Coverage to Informal Workers: A Systematic Review of Health Financing Schemes in Low- and Middle-Income Countries in Southeast Asia
authors: Andrea Hannah Kaiser, Niccolò Rotigliano, Steffen Flessa, Björn Ekman, Jesper Sundewall
description: Systematic review of health financing schemes implemented to extend UHC to informal workers in Southeast Asia, a region characterized by high prevalence of informal employment.

title: Moving toward Universal Health Coverage with a National Health Insurance Program: A Scoping Review of Experiences in Eleven Low- and Lower-Middle-Income Countries
authors: Katya Galactionova, Elizabeth Kalesha, Abena Asumadu-Sakyi, Fabrizio Tediosi
description: Assesses strategies adopted by low- and lower-middle-income countries with a national health insurance scheme to expand UHC, examining variations in progress across eleven countries.

title: Achieving Universal Health Coverage in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: Challenges for Policy Post-Pandemic and Beyond
authors: Oyeli Ochie, Olalekan Olasehinde, Rebecca Adegboyega
description: Narrative review of 118 peer-reviewed and grey literature sources examining UHC challenges in LMICs, including weak health systems, financing gaps, and epidemiological transitions exacerbated by COVID-19.

title: Tracking Universal Health Coverage: 2023 Global Monitoring Report
authors: World Health Organization, World Bank
description: Joint WHO-World Bank global monitoring report tracking progress toward UHC, concluding the world is off track for significant progress by 2030 with persistent gaps in service coverage and financial protection.

title: Workload and Emerging Challenges of Community Health Workers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Mixed-Methods Systematic Review
authors: Tegbar Yigzaw, Wondwosen Shiferaw, Frehiwot Berhane, Mengistu Hailemariam
description: Systematic review summarizing community health workers' perceived workload in LMICs, finding that excessive workload affects productivity and retention of CHWs in remote and underserved areas.

title: Community Health Workers and COVID-19: Cross-Country Evidence on Their Roles, Experiences, Challenges and Adaptive Strategies
authors: Meenakshi Gautham, Kok Maryse, Kerry Scott, Shivam Gupta, Maryse C Kok
description: Cross-country study examining CHWs' contributions during COVID-19 in India, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Sierra Leone, Kenya, and Ethiopia, analyzing their frontline roles, challenges, and adaptive strategies.

title: Task Shifting Healthcare Services in the Post-COVID World: A Scoping Review
authors: Emily Liang, Arjun Mitra, Sonia Abi Jaoude, Veronica Vargas
description: Scoping review of task shifting and task sharing approaches that move tasks from highly trained professionals to other providers, examining evidence and implications for health service delivery in LMICs.

title: Regional Overview of Food Security and Nutrition 2020: Asia and the Pacific
authors: FAO, UNICEF, WFP, WHO
description: Joint UN report on food security and nutrition in Asia and the Pacific, assessing progress toward SDG 2 targets and noting the expected impact of COVID-19 disruptions on all forms of malnutrition.

title: Factors Associated with Wasting Among Children Under Five Years Old in South Asia: Implications for Action
authors: Nita Dalmiya, Harriet Torlesse, Victor Aguayo
description: Analysis of factors associated with child wasting across South Asia finding that low maternal BMI is a significant predictor and boys are more likely to be wasted than girls in Afghanistan, Bangladesh, India, and Pakistan.

title: Contribution of Malnutrition to Infant and Child Deaths in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia
authors: Cynthia Boschi-Pinto, Emily R Smith, Jonathon L Simon, Myo Myo Mon, Alka Dev
description: Study using postmortem data from seven LMICs revealing that malnutrition plays a significant role in the causal chain of 40% of deaths among children under 5 years of age in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia.

title: Socioeconomic Inequalities in Access to Maternal Healthcare in South-Asian Countries: A Systematic Review
authors: Parveen Ali, Rubab Zahra, Shehla Naeem, Sana Waqas
description: Systematic review of 55 studies from India, Nepal, Bangladesh, Afghanistan, and Pakistan showing that maternal healthcare access is linked to economic status, education, and women's autonomy in patriarchal family structures.

title: COVID-19 and Mental Health in 8 Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Prospective Cohort Study
authors: Tassew Woldehanna, Anja Benshaul-Tolonen, Manuela Angelucci, Sandra Favara, Marta Favara
description: Prospective cohort study of 21,162 individuals across 8 LMICs in Asia, Africa, and South America, finding that depression symptoms increased significantly (0.29 SD) in the first 4 months following COVID-19 onset.

title: Mental Health Policy Implementation in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Realist Review Protocol
authors: Ruth Trainor, Florence Baingana, Assegid Debebe
description: Protocol for a realist synthesis explaining how, why, for whom, and under what health system conditions certain mental health policy implementation strategies work or fail in LMICs.

title: Measures to Strengthen Primary Health-Care Systems in Low- and Middle-Income Countries
authors: Shannon Barkley, Ernesto Bascolo, Hernan Montenegro, Shannon McManus
description: WHO Bulletin article identifying priorities for improving primary health care in LMICs, including community engagement and adapting PHC to evolving population needs for achieving UHC by 2030.

title: Primary Health Care on the Road to Universal Health Coverage: 2019 Global Monitoring Report
authors: World Health Organization, World Bank
description: WHO-World Bank monitoring report examining PHC service coverage indicators across income groups and regions, released ahead of the UN High-Level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage.

title: Self-Management of Chronic, Non-Communicable Diseases in South Asian Settings: A Systematic Mixed-Studies Review
authors: Farooq Siddiqui, Omara Dogar, Mona Kanaan, Kamran Siddiqi
description: Systematic review finding that age, education, income, knowledge, social support, self-efficacy, and affordability of medicine are key determinants of chronic NCD self-management in South Asia.

title: Social Determinants of Health: The Role of Effective Communication in the COVID-19 Pandemic in Developing Countries
authors: John Osita Ataguba
description: Open access paper in Global Health Action arguing that effective crisis communication is among the most critical social determinants of health for reducing COVID-19 morbidity and relieving pressure on weak health systems in developing countries.

title: Social Determinants of Infectious Diseases in South Asia
authors: Bir Rawal, Rajesh Manandhar, Sameer Dixit
description: PMC open access paper examining social determinants of infectious diseases in South Asia with special reference to HIV and tuberculosis, concluding that addressing social factors is essential for sustainable intervention.

title: Social Determinants of Health, Report of a Regional Consultation, Colombo, Sri Lanka
authors: World Health Organization, Regional Office for South-East Asia
description: WHO South-East Asia regional consultation report on social determinants of health, examining health inequities driven by socioeconomic position and the double burden of disease in the region.

title: Demographic, Socioeconomic and Regional Disparities in the Coverage of Water, Sanitation and Hygiene Facilities in Four South Asian Countries
authors: Md Hasibul Islam Jitu, Mohammad Shahed Masud
description: PLOS ONE study using MICS data from Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Nepal, and Pakistan to examine WASH facility coverage disparities and identify significant socioeconomic and demographic factors.

title: Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene (WASH) Factors and the Incidence of Communicable Diseases in Urban Bangladesh: Evidence from Municipal Areas
authors: Md Nuruzzaman Khan, Raaj Kishore Biswas, Shuva Das
description: PLOS NTDs study finding that nearly one in four participants in urban Bangladesh reported communicable diseases, with diarrhoea most common and poor hand hygiene showing the strongest disease association.

title: Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH): The Evolution of a Global Health and Development Sector
authors: Sara de Wit, Euphrasia Luseka, David Bradley, Joe Brown, Jayant Bhagwan, Barbara Evans, Matthew C Freeman, Guy Howard, Isha Ray, Ian Ross, Sheillah Simiyu, Oliver Cumming, Clare I R Chandler
description: BMJ Global Health open access article examining the evolution of the global WASH sector, noting that universal WASH access by 2030 under SDG 6 remains a distant prospect in many countries.

title: Progress on Household Drinking Water, Sanitation and Hygiene 2000-2020: Five Years into the SDGs
authors: WHO, UNICEF
description: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme report presenting updated global and regional WASH estimates, serving as the leading source of comparable progress data for SDG 6 monitoring.

title: Essential Childhood Immunization in 43 Low- and Middle-Income Countries: Analysis of Spatial Trends and Socioeconomic Inequalities in Vaccine Coverage
authors: Anna Dimitrova, Jayanta Bhattacharya, Nirmala Naidoo, Ties Boerma
description: PLOS Medicine open access analysis of spatial trends and socioeconomic inequalities in essential childhood immunization coverage across 43 LMICs, revealing persistent wealth and geographic disparities.

title: Vaccine Equity in Low and Middle Income Countries: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
authors: Abimbola Olaniran, Aduragbemi Banke-Thomas, Helen Smith, Susan Tull
description: Systematic review highlighting that global DTP3 coverage stagnated at 85% since 2010, with 14 million infants missing initial doses in 2019, driven by wealth, education, gender, and geographic inequalities.

title: Digital Health Interventions for Improving Access to Primary Care in India: A Scoping Review
authors: Smisha Agarwal, Yumna Vahanvaty, Manushi Patel, Olakunle Alonge
description: PLOS Global Public Health scoping review of digital health interventions to improve primary care access in India, examining telemedicine and mobile health approaches for vulnerable populations.

title: World Development Report 2004: Making Services Work for Poor People
authors: World Bank
description: World Bank flagship report arguing that broad improvements in welfare require putting poor people at the center of service provision, proposing frameworks for monitoring providers, amplifying voice, and strengthening incentives.

title: Monitoring Progress towards Universal Health Coverage at Country and Global Levels
authors: Margaret E Kruk, Anna D Gage, Catherine Arsenault, Keely Jordan, Hannah H Leslie, Sanam Roder-DeWan, Olusoji Adeyi, Pierre Barker, Bernadette Daelmans, Svetlana V Doubova
description: PLOS Medicine framework paper proposing methods for monitoring UHC progress at country and global levels, examining both service coverage and financial protection indicators.
